title: Add explicit chain-of-thought scratchpad for planning and review agents
source: Chain-of-thought / 'let the model think before answering' research and Anthropic extended-thinking guidance for complex reasoning tasks
description: Planning (ticket breakdown) and review agents produce better decompositions and catch more defects when instructed to reason step-by-step in a dedicated scratchpad before emitting the final structured answer. Keep the reasoning inside a <thinking> block that the parser strips, so observability isn't polluted but quality improves.
proposed_changes: For planning/review role prompts in orchestrate.py, add an instruction to 'First reason inside <thinking>...</thinking> about edge cases, dependencies, and risks, then give your final answer in the required <answer> JSON block'. In delegate.py's parser, strip and discard <thinking> content (optionally log it to the roro event stream at debug level) and parse only the <answer> block. Gate this behind a role flag so cheap one-shot Meeseeks tasks skip the extra tokens.
reject_reason: Apply failed: Evolve delegation failed: (no stderr)
